claimText: Oilzapper and Oilivorous-S are bioremediation technologies developed by The Energy and Resources Institute (TERI) that use bacteria to convert harmful hydrocarbons into carbon dioxide and water.
evidenceExcerpt: Oilzapper and Oilivorous-S are bioremediation technologies developed by TERI that use bacteria to convert hydrocarbons into carbon dioxide and water.
examUse: Statement-based questions on environmental technologies or correct-pair matching (Technology vs Developer/Function).
distractorHooks: Developed by DRDO or CSIR instead of TERI
Uses chemical dispersants instead of bacteria
Converts hydrocarbons into solid waste instead of CO2 and water

claimText: Milkweed plant fiber, commonly found in Rajasthan, acts as an effective sorbent material for cleaning oil spills.
evidenceExcerpt: Milkweed plant fiber can be effectively used as a sorbent material for oil spills.
examUse: Statement-based questions on natural materials used in disaster management.
distractorHooks: Used as a chemical dispersant instead of a sorbent
Found exclusively in marine environments instead of Rajasthan

claimText: In oil spill management, oil booms physically isolate the spill area using floating barriers, whereas skimmers are machines that scrape the oil from the water surface.
evidenceExcerpt: Oil booms physically isolate spills, while skimmers scrape oil from the water surface.
examUse: Comparison or correct-pair questions distinguishing between different physical oil spill cleanup methods.
distractorHooks: Swapping the functions of booms and skimmers
Describing booms or skimmers as biological or chemical treatments rather than physical tools
